description: Sync your Kindle book highlights using your Amazon login or uploading your My Clippings file.

manifest.name: Kindle Highlights

name: Kindle Highlights

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
## Fixes & polish
- Keep the selected sync method even if you cancel mid-sync
- Keep the sync activity log scrolled to the latest entry
- Improve first-run/empty-library messaging
- Improve idle library placeholders and fill rows when you have only a few books

(Theme-aware ribbon/status indicators and detailed sync logging remain from 2.3.0.)

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
## Highlights
- Cancel in-progress sync with clear cancelling/cancelled UI
- Persistent sync indicators (status bar text + ribbon pulse)
- Detailed activity log with per-step timing and Copy button
- Improved idle dashboard (animated library view, sync method selector, ignored patterns + library folder)

## Notes
- This is a UI/UX improvement release (no breaking data format changes).

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
## What's New

### Improved Book Ignore List
- **Substring matching**: Type just part of a book title (e.g. "Stormlight" instead of the full Amazon title) to exclude it from syncing. Case-insensitive.
- **Right-click to ignore**: Right-click any synced Kindle note → "Kindle: Ignore this book" to add it to your ignore list instantly.
- **Right-click to ignore and delete**: Right-click → "Kindle: Ignore and delete this book" to move the file to trash and prevent re-syncing. Includes a confirmation modal.
- **Better settings UI**: The ignore list textarea now has clearer description text, helpful placeholder examples, and a larger input area.

### Updated README
- Full rewrite covering all v2.x features: Amazon regions, Obsidian Properties format, sync on startup, templating variables, book ignore list, and safe filename handling.
- New Settings reference table.

### Documentation
- Added manual validation convention to AGENTS.md.
- Updated test count (134 tests).

### Full Changelog
- Switch ignore list from exact to substring matching
- Add "Kindle: Ignore this book" file menu command
- Add "Kindle: Ignore and delete this book" with confirmation modal
- Use Obsidian-native Modal API for delete confirmation
- Prefix all file menu items with "Kindle:" for clarity
- Rewrite README for v2.x features
- 7 new unit tests for ignore list filtering
releases.name: v2.2.0

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
## What's New

### Bug Fix: Author Parsing (#306)
Fixed `authorsLastNames` and related template variables for books with authors in `LastName, FirstName` format (common on Amazon). Previously, `"Kegan, Robert and Lahey, Lisa Laskow"` would incorrectly split on both commas and "and", destroying the name pairing.

### New Feature: Book Ignore List (#309)
You can now exclude specific books from syncing. Go to **Settings → Ignored books** and enter book titles (one per line, case-insensitive). Works for both Amazon sync and My Clippings sync.

### Full Changelog
- Fix `parseAuthors` to correctly handle `LastName, FirstName and LastName2, FirstName2` format
- Add book ignore list setting with textarea UI in plugin settings
- Ignore filtering applies to both Amazon and My Clippings sync paths
releases.name: v2.1.0

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
## v2.0.1 — Filename Sanitization Fix

Fixes issues where book titles or author names with special characters caused sync failures, missing files, or plugin hangs.

### Bug Fixes
- **Obsidian-specific filename sanitization** — Characters forbidden by Obsidian (`#`, `^`, `[`, `]`) are now stripped from generated filenames (#220, #266, #287)
- **Meaningful separator replacement** — Colons and forward slashes in titles are now replaced with ` - ` instead of being silently stripped. E.g. `"Deep Work: Rules for Success"` → `"Deep Work - Rules for Success.md"` instead of `"Deep Work Rules for Success.md"`
- **Accented character preservation** — Characters like `é`, `ñ`, `ü`, `ö` in author names and titles are fully preserved
- **Double space cleanup** — Multiple consecutive spaces (left behind after character stripping) are collapsed into single spaces

### Affected Issues
- #220 — Books with `/` in title not recognized from My Clippings
- #266 — Sync hangs on books with special characters in title
- #287 — Accented characters in author names hinder syncing

### Testing
If you were affected by any of the above issues, please update to v2.0.1 and let us know if the problem is resolved.
releases.name: v2.0.1 — Filename Sanitization Fix

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
## 🎉 v2.0.0 — Community Revival

After a long hiatus, the Kindle Highlights plugin is back! This release incorporates **14 community pull requests** contributed over the past 3 years. Huge thanks to all contributors who kept the project alive.

### ✨ New Features
- **Obsidian Properties format** — Highlights now use Obsidian's native properties/frontmatter format with improved performance (#328)
- **Migration command** — Use `Kindle: Migrate properties to new format` from the command palette to convert existing files
- **New template variables** — `publicationDate` and `lastAnnotatedDate` available in file name templates (#254, #282)
- **Amazon.nl support** — Added Netherlands region (#278)
- **Amazon.ca support** — Added Canada region (#294)

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fixed notes not displaying after sync (#322)
- Fixed crash on sync at startup (#324)
- Fixed `startSync()` missing parameter (#257)
- Fixed book cover images — now uses higher resolution 1024px images (#246)
- Fixed product page URLs to match selected Amazon region (#279)
- Fixed timeout for large libraries — increased to 30 seconds (#310)
- Fixed author URL scraping (#280)
- Fixed Amazon logout exception handling (#323)

### 🔧 Maintenance
- Updated dependencies, reducing vulnerabilities from 27 to 3 (#326)
- Updated CI/CD to Node 20 and modern GitHub Actions
- Version bump to 2.0.0

### 📝 Notes
- This release is **backward compatible** — existing highlight files will continue to work
- To use the new Obsidian properties format, run the migration command from the command palette
- The plugin now supports 10 Amazon regions: .com, .co.uk, .co.jp, .de, .fr, .es, .it, .in, .nl, .ca

### Contributors
Thank you to everyone who submitted PRs during the hiatus! Your contributions made this release possible.
releases.name: v2.0.0 — Community Revival

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
Introduction of a key new feature to the plugin — resync of highlights for books that have been previously synced before.

This introduces a breaking change. For resync to work, the plugin needs to store state information. This is done via:

- Frontmatter — stores state data required for the plugin to determine how to resync
- Block references — a block references above every highlights e.g. `^234235`. Allows the plugin to uniquely identify an individual highlight in order to figure out where to place new highlights

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:
Implemented feature request https://github.com/hadynz/obsidian-kindle-plugin/issues/11.

Added a new toggle to plugin to download extra meta data for a book. When a user does a sync via Amazon, and the book has an ASIN, then plugin will scrape the following extra data points and make available in rendering template of a highlight:

* ISBN
* Print length
* Publication date
* Publisher
* Book author URL

releases.author: hadynz

releases.author: hadynz
releases.description:

* Sync modal is aware of upload clippings file mode and shows the right text (https://github.com/hadynz/obsidian-kindle-plugin/issues/16)

Bug fixes
* Warn instead of hard failing if a user note entry is not preceded by a highlight as expected (https://github.com/hadynz/obsidian-kindle-plugin/issues/16)
* Handle empty lines in clippings file that may precede title/author and metadata line entries (https://github.com/hadynz/obsidian-kindle-plugin/issues/15)
